title: Colour-blind-safe palette + grayscale/ink-saver design system
context: As the project expands from one manipulative family to four, a consistent colour and visual-encoding system is essential. The system must be colour-blind-safe (the most common form of colour blindness affects roughly 8% of males), must degrade gracefully to grayscale on black-and-white printers, and must provide an ink-saver outline mode for low-ink situations. Without a formal design system, individual generator modules will make inconsistent choices and some outputs will be inaccessible.
objective: Define and publish a colour-blind-safe colour palette and a full grayscale/ink-saver design system for use across all manipulative families, with accessibility review sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines a primary colour palette of at least 6 named colours that passes Okabe-Ito colour-blind simulation (distinguishable under protanopia, deuteranopia, and tritanopia).
Defines luminance values for each colour such that grayscale renderings (via luminance mapping) preserve distinguishability between any two colours used together in a single manipulative.
Defines an ink-saver mode: outline-only with hatching or pattern fills replacing solid colours; patterns are distinguishable in black-and-white.
Palette and patterns are specified in a machine-readable form usable by the generator engine (e.g. JSON or TypeScript constants).
Reviewed and signed off by a qualified accessibility reviewer; sign-off recorded in the document.
resources: tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide v1 (colour mode requirements)
output: A design-system document (design/colour-palette-design-system.md) with a companion machine-readable palette file (design/palette.json), CC-BY-4.0, with accessibility reviewer sign-off recorded.
